claim: CAB disease-architecture regimes predict future assertion meaning drift across rolling historical origins.
publication_safe_note: Supported as historical prospective emulation / temporal backtesting; see model comparison and regime signatures.

claim: Pathogenic meaning evolves over time according to disease architecture.
publication_safe_note: Supported by longitudinal assertion evolution rates stratified by regime.

claim: Anchored regimes show persistent self-loop stability.
publication_safe_note: Supported when phenotype-anchored monogenic self-loop rates exceed underresolved/modifier regimes.

claim: Underresolved/modifier/structural-overlap regimes show persistent non-portability or repair/review routing.
publication_safe_note: Supported by unsupported reuse and review_repair_rate columns.

claim: CAB review queues enrich future boundary-crossing assertions across historical eras.
publication_safe_note: Supported by review queue enrichment results across eras.
This package is a 10-year rolling-origin validation / historical prospective emulation, not true prospective validation.
Endpoints are ClinVar assertion meaning drift, not patient outcomes.
No patient-level risk endpoint is modeled.
ClinVar changes may reflect many processes outside CAB disease architecture.
CAB routes review/repair queues and does not replace expert adjudication.
